basic_details.job_title: DHFWS Udupi Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for 18 Junior Laboratory Technical Officer, Pharmacy Officer Posts
basic_details.organization_name: District Health & Family Welfare Office (DHFWS), Udupi District, Government of Karnataka
basic_details.total_vacancies: Junior Laboratory Technical Officer (05), Pharmacy Officer (13)
basic_details.short_information: The District Health & Family Welfare Office (DHFWS), Udupi District, Karnataka, has invited applications for 18 contract-based posts - 5 Junior Laboratory Technical Officer and 13 Pharmacy Officer - to be filled for a period of one year. Eligible candidates must obtain the application form from udupi.nic.in and submit it, along with the required documents, to the District Health and Family Welfare Officer's office on or before 21 July 2026, 5:00 PM.

important_dates.interview_date: To be announced later, after publication of the eligible candidates' list

application_fee.details: The DHFWS Udupi notification does not mention any application fee for these posts. Candidates can obtain the application form from udupi.nic.in and submit it without making any payment.

age_limit.age_details: Contract basis, for a period of one year
age_limit.details: A single age limit applies to both posts under this notification, with no separate figure mentioned for reserved categories. Candidates should confirm they meet this limit before applying. | Candidates should not exceed 45 years of age. The notification does not mention any age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC or other reserved categories.

qualification.details: Each of the two posts has its own specific educational qualification requirement, and both require the qualification to be obtained only from institutions recognized by the Government of Karnataka. Pharmacy Officer candidates additionally need mandatory council registration. | Diploma in Medical Laboratory Technology (DMLT), obtained only from institutions recognized by the Government of Karnataka. | D.Pharma qualification, obtained only from institutions recognized by the Government of Karnataka. D.Pharma candidates must be mandatorily registered with the Karnataka Pharmacy Council.

salary_details.details: Both posts under this recruitment carry a consolidated monthly salary tied to statutory minimum wage rates rather than a fixed rupee figure. Here is what the notification specifies. | Monthly consolidated salary: as per the minimum wage fixed by the Labour Department, applicable to both Junior Laboratory Technical Officer and Pharmacy Officer posts. The notification does not mention a specific rupee amount; the minimum wage rate notified by the Karnataka Labour Department will apply.

selection_process: Selection for these contract posts follows government roster and merit rules
with an interview stage after screening of eligible candidates. Here is what the notification specifies about the process.

Candidates submit the filled application form along with required documents to the DHFWO office by the deadline. The office reviews applications and publishes a list of eligible candidates on udupi.nic.in and the office notice board. The interview date will be announced later to the shortlisted/eligible candidates. Recruitment will be carried out following roster and merit rules. If a suitable candidate is not available in a reserved category
the next candidate in the roster point will be considered for that category.
how_to_apply: Applying for these posts is entirely offline - candidates download the application form online but must submit the completed physical form to the DHFWO office. Here are the steps to apply.
Visit the official website udupi.nic.in and download the application form for the post of Lab Technician or Pharmacist.
Fill in the Contact Information section: full name, address for communication, contact number, and a compulsory email address.

Fill in the Personal Information section: date of birth, gender, religion, caste category, and whether you are a Kannada medium candidate, rural candidate, or physically handicapped candidate (attaching supporting documents where applicable).
Fill in the Educational Qualification section with details of your qualifications, attaching the marks card and relevant documents for each.
Attach your registration certificate (KSPC for Pharmacy Officer or the relevant Paramedical Board registration for Lab Technician).
Attach a recent passport-size photograph and sign the declaration confirming the information is correct.
Submit the complete application with all self-attested documents to the District Health and Family Welfare Officer's office, Udupi, on or before 21 July 2026, 5:00 PM.
For more information, candidates can contact the office at 0820-2525566.
important_instructions: Candidates should keep the following points in mind, since they directly affect how the recruitment and reservation process will be carried out.

Recruitment will be conducted following roster and merit rules as applicable under Karnataka government norms. If a candidate is not available in a particular reserved category, the next roster point candidate will be considered in that category's place. The list of eligible candidates will be published on udupi.nic.in and on the office notice board. The interview date for eligible candidates will be communicated separately after the eligibility list is published. All submitted documents must be self-attested copies.

rows.information: District Health & Family Welfare Office (DHFWS), Udupi District, Government of Karnataka
Junior Laboratory Technical Officer (05), Pharmacy Officer (13)
Contract basis, for a period of one year
Offline (application form downloaded from udupi.nic.in, submitted physically to the DHFWO office)

content_sections.heading: Important Dates
content_sections.text: This recruitment has a single, firm deadline for application submission, with the interview date to be communicated later to eligible candidates. Candidates should note that this is a time-of-day deadline, not just a date-based one. The list of eligible candidates will be published on udupi.nic.in and on the office notice board, and the interview date will be communicated to candidates subsequently.

rows.event: Last Date for Submission of Application

rows.date: To be announced later, after publication of the eligible candidates' list

content_sections.heading: Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: Out of the total 18 posts sanctioned for Udupi district, the majority are for Pharmacy Officer, with a smaller number reserved for Junior Laboratory Technical Officer. These posts were approved by the Director, Health & Family Welfare Services, Bengaluru, via order number e-Office-1840947 dated 15.06.2026.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria: Educational Qualification
content_sections.text: Each of the two posts has its own specific educational qualification requirement, and both require the qualification to be obtained only from institutions recognized by the Government of Karnataka. Pharmacy Officer candidates additionally need mandatory council registration. Diploma in Medical Laboratory Technology (DMLT), obtained only from institutions recognized by the Government of Karnataka. D.Pharma qualification, obtained only from institutions recognized by the Government of Karnataka. D.Pharma candidates must be mandatorily registered with the Karnataka Pharmacy Council .

content_sections.heading: Age Limit
content_sections.text: A single age limit applies to both posts under this notification, with no separate figure mentioned for reserved categories. Candidates should confirm they meet this limit before applying. Candidates should not exceed 45 years of age . The notification does not mention any age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC or other reserved categories.

content_sections.heading: Salary
content_sections.text: Both posts under this recruitment carry a consolidated monthly salary tied to statutory minimum wage rates rather than a fixed rupee figure. Here is what the notification specifies. Monthly consolidated salary: as per the minimum wage fixed by the Labour Department , applicable to both Junior Laboratory Technical Officer and Pharmacy Officer posts. The notification does not mention a specific rupee amount; the minimum wage rate notified by the Karnataka Labour Department will apply.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: The DHFWS Udupi notification does not mention any application fee for these posts. Candidates can obtain the application form from udupi.nic.in and submit it without making any payment.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection for these contract posts follows government roster and merit rules, with an interview stage after screening of eligible candidates. Here is what the notification specifies about the process. Candidates submit the filled application form along with required documents to the DHFWO office by the deadline. The office reviews applications and publishes a list of eligible candidates on udupi.nic.in and the office notice board. The interview date will be announced later to the shortlisted/eligible candidates. Recruitment will be carried out following roster and merit rules. If a suitable candidate is not available in a reserved category, the next candidate in the roster point will be considered for that category.

content_sections.heading: Required Documents
content_sections.text: The documents required differ slightly between the two posts, mainly in terms of the qualification marksheets and council registration certificate. All documents must be self-attested before submission. SSLC marks card Diploma in Medical Laboratory Technology (DMLT) marks cards Paramedical Board registration certificate Latest caste certificate Kannada language certificate Rural candidate certificate Physically handicapped certificate, if applicable Aadhaar card SSLC marks card D.Pharma marks cards Karnataka Pharmacy Council registration certificate Latest caste certificate Kannada language certificate Rural candidate certificate Physically handicapped certificate, if applicable Aadhaar card All the above documents must be self-attested before submission with the application form.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply
content_sections.text: Applying for these posts is entirely offline - candidates download the application form online but must submit the completed physical form to the DHFWO office. Here are the steps to apply. Visit the official website udupi.nic.in and download the application form for the post of Lab Technician or Pharmacist. Fill in the Contact Information section: full name, address for communication, contact number, and a compulsory email address. Fill in the Personal Information section: date of birth, gender, religion, caste category, and whether you are a Kannada medium candidate, rural candidate, or physically handicapped candidate (attaching supporting documents where applicable). Fill in the Educational Qualification section with details of your qualifications, attaching the marks card and relevant documents for each. Attach your registration certificate (KSPC for Pharmacy Officer or the relevant Paramedical Board registration for Lab Technician). Attach a recent passport-size photograph and sign the declaration confirming the information is correct. Submit the complete application with all self-attested documents to the District Health and Family Welfare Officer's office, Udupi , on or before 21 July 2026, 5:00 PM . For more information, candidates can contact the office at 0820-2525566 .

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Candidates should keep the following points in mind, since they directly affect how the recruitment and reservation process will be carried out. Recruitment will be conducted following roster and merit rules as applicable under Karnataka government norms. If a candidate is not available in a particular reserved category, the next roster point candidate will be considered in that category's place. The list of eligible candidates will be published on udupi.nic.in and on the office notice board. The interview date for eligible candidates will be communicated separately after the eligibility list is published. All submitted documents must be self-attested copies.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for DHFWS Udupi Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the application is 21 July 2026, before 5:00 PM.

faq.question: Q2. How many total vacancies are there in this DHFWS Udupi notification?
faq.answer: There are 18 total vacancies - 5 for Junior Laboratory Technical Officer and 13 for Pharmacy Officer.

faq.question: Q3. What is the eligibility for the Junior Laboratory Technical Officer post?
faq.answer: Candidates need a Diploma in Medical Laboratory Technology (DMLT) obtained from an institution recognized by the Government of Karnataka.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility for the Pharmacy Officer post?
faq.answer: Candidates need a D.Pharma qualification from a Karnataka Government-recognized institution and must be registered with the Karnataka Pharmacy Council.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for these posts?
faq.answer: Candidates should not exceed 45 years of age; no age relaxation is mentioned in the notification.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary for Junior Laboratory Technical Officer and Pharmacy Officer posts?
faq.answer: Both posts carry a consolidated monthly salary as per the minimum wage fixed by the Labour Department; no fixed rupee figure is mentioned in the notification.

faq.question: Q7. How can candidates apply for DHFWS Udupi Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must download the application form from udupi.nic.in, fill it with the required details and self-attested documents, and submit it physically to the DHFWO office by 21 July 2026.
